name: Amara ibn Salkhab
title: Azdi Helper
shortDescription: A brave man from the Azd tribe in Kufa who intended to assist Muslim ibn Aqeel but was arrested and executed.
fullStory:
Amara ibn Salkhab al-Azdi was a brave, prominent man and a sincere Shia of Kufa. He had deep devotion to the Ahl al-Bayt (AS) and pledged allegiance to Muslim ibn Aqeel upon his arrival in Kufa.

When the situation in Kufa changed and Ibn Ziyad initiated a reign of terror, Amara ibn Salkhab took up arms to join Muslim ibn Aqeel's uprising.

On his way to help Muslim, he was identified and arrested by Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad's forces and taken to the tyrannical governor of Kufa. Ibn Ziyad demanded that he call his people (the Azd tribe) to obey Yazid and abandon the uprising, but Amara refused to bear this shame.

Unable to make him surrender, Ibn Ziyad ordered him to be beheaded among his tribe to serve as a warning to others. Thus, Amara sacrificed his life before the Imam reached Karbala.
quoteArabic: رَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ، إِنَّهُ قُتِلَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ...
quote: (About him:) He was martyred in Kufa before the event of Karbala in the path of assisting Wilayah and Muslim ibn Aqeel.
